findings.description: Op-amp circuit in comparator_or_open_loop configuration

findings.summary: U1 pin *XRES (reset) missing pull-up resistor
findings.description: Pin *XRES on U1 (WLC1115-68LQXQ) is connected to net reset but has no pull-up resistor. This pin type typically requires an external pull-up to a power rail for correct operation.
findings.recommendation: Add a 4.7k-10k pull-up resistor from reset to the appropriate power rail.

fix_params.basis: Pin *XRES is open-drain/input type requiring pull-up

report_context.impact: Pin may float or bus may not function without pull-up

findings.summary: +5V has no declared source
findings.description: Net +5V carries power_in pins but has no power_out pin, no PWR_FLAG, no regulator output mapping, and no bridged solder jumper path to a sourced net.
findings.recommendation: Add a PWR_FLAG to declare the rail as externally powered (e.g. from a connector) or trace the rail back to a regulator output. If the source lives on another sheet, promote the net name to a global label.

report_context.impact: Rail has no source visible to the analyser; likely a wiring gap or missing PWR_FLAG.

findings.summary: Sourcing blocker: BOM has <50% MPN coverage (3/35 unique parts). Board is not pre-fab ready.
findings.description: Sourcing audit: 3 of 35 unique BOM lines (grouped by value + footprint) carry a manufacturer part number (8.6%). Pre-fab readiness requires 100% coverage; production assembly requires all parts sourceable via one of the supported distributors.
findings.recommendation: Populate the MPN field on the listed refs (use the digikey / mouser / lcsc / element14 skill to search by value/footprint when the MPN isn't already known).

report_context.impact: Pre-fab gate: board cannot be ordered until MPN coverage reaches 100%.

findings.summary: Lifecycle audit not run — --lifecycle flag not passed
findings.description: Component lifecycle / obsolescence audit was not run this session. Reason: --lifecycle flag not passed (run with --lifecycle to query DigiKey / Mouser / LCSC / element14 for obsolescence + operating-temp coverage; requires network + API keys). The review cannot report active / NRND / EOL / obsolete status, last-time-buy windows, or operating-temperature coverage for any BOM part. Treat the lifecycle section of the report as 'not performed'.
findings.recommendation: Run with --lifecycle (requires DIGIKEY_CLIENT_ID / MOUSER_SEARCH_API_KEY / ELEMENT14_API_KEY env vars; LCSC needs no auth) to populate this section. If intentionally skipped, note it explicitly in the report's 'Not Performed' section.

report_context.impact: No obsolescence / temperature-grade evidence available.

observations: +5V: highest SRF is 22.51 MHz — consider adding small (100pF-1nF) MLCC for >100 MHz coverage
VDDD: highest SRF is 22.51 MHz — consider adding small (100pF-1nF) MLCC for >100 MHz coverage
VBB_1: highest SRF is 22.51 MHz — consider adding small (100pF-1nF) MLCC for >100 MHz coverage
PVIN_0: highest SRF is 1.52 MHz — consider adding small (100pF-1nF) MLCC for >100 MHz coverage
15 single-use passive values — consider standardizing to reduce BOM line items
Key nets without test points: 4 i2c, 5 power_rail, 1 reset

issues: Pull-up 5100Ω may not meet VOL<0.4V spec at fast mode (6mA sink): max 67Ω
observations: SDA pull-up R21=5.1k valid for standard mode but too high for fast mode (max 4.7K)
SCL pull-up R20=5.1k valid for standard mode but too high for fast mode (max 4.7K)

top_issues.summary: Sourcing blocker: BOM has <50% MPN coverage (3/35 unique parts). Board is not pre-fab ready.
top_issues.description: Sourcing audit: 3 of 35 unique BOM lines (grouped by value + footprint) carry a manufacturer part number (8.6%). Pre-fab readiness requires 100% coverage; production assembly requires all parts sourceable via one of the supported distributors.
top_issues.recommendation: Populate the MPN field on the listed refs (use the digikey / mouser / lcsc / element14 skill to search by value/footprint when the MPN isn't already known).

report_context.impact: Pre-fab gate: board cannot be ordered until MPN coverage reaches 100%.

top_issues.summary: No datasheets directory found (6/87 parts have MPNs). Sync datasheets before making pin-level claims.
top_issues.recommendation: Run datasheet sync via the digikey / mouser / lcsc / element14 skill (which one depends on the user's distributor access and API credentials). A ./datasheets/ directory with PDFs and a manifest.json enables pin-level verification in the next analyzer run.

report_context.impact: Review claims must stay at consistency level until datasheets land.

top_issues.description: Net '__unnamed_14' has exactly one pin: U1.14 (VBRG_DIS), pin_type='power_in'. Verify the intended target — if this pin should connect somewhere, the wire/label is missing.
top_issues.recommendation: Check the schematic at the referenced pin — add the missing wire or place a no-connect marker if the pin is intentionally floating.

top_issues.summary: Sourcing blocker: BOM has <50% MPN coverage (3/35 unique parts). Board is not pre-fab ready.
top_issues.recommendation: Populate the MPN field on the listed refs (use the digikey / mouser / lcsc / element14 skill to search by value/footprint when the MPN isn't already known).

top_issues.summary: No datasheets directory found (6/87 parts have MPNs). Sync datasheets before making pin-level claims.
top_issues.recommendation: Run datasheet sync via the digikey / mouser / lcsc / element14 skill (which one depends on the user's distributor access and API credentials). A ./datasheets/ directory with PDFs and a manifest.json enables pin-level verification in the next analyzer run.

top_issues.summary: U1 pin *XRES (reset) missing pull-up resistor
top_issues.recommendation: Add a 4.7k-10k pull-up resistor from reset to the appropriate power rail.

top_issues.summary: +5V has no declared source
top_issues.recommendation: Add a PWR_FLAG to declare the rail as externally powered (e.g. from a connector) or trace the rail back to a regulator output. If the source lives on another sheet, promote the net name to a global label.
